name: Sunni Insurgency — IED Campaign (2003–2008)
detail: Following de-Ba'athification and army dissolution, Sunni Arab insurgents (former military, AQI, nationalist groups) waged asymmetric warfare using improvised explosive devices (IEDs), vehicle-borne IEDs (VBIEDs), and ambushes. IEDs killed 63% of all US combat fatalities. Iran supplied Explosively Formed Penetrators (EFPs) to Shia militias. At peak (2006), ~100 attacks/day across Iraq.

name: Al-Askari Mosque Bombing — AQI Sectarian Campaign
detail: Al-Qaeda in Iraq bombed the golden dome of Samarra's Al-Askari Mosque on February 22, 2006, triggering nationwide sectarian violence. AQI under Zarqawi pursued a deliberate strategy of provoking Shia-Sunni civil war to destabilize the US-backed government and create a Sunni caliphate. The attack succeeded: sectarian killings soared to 3,000+/month; 2.7M Iraqis displaced.

name: Iran-Backed Shia Militia Attacks on US Forces
detail: Iran-backed Shia militias — particularly Kata'ib Hezbollah and Asa'ib Ahl al-Haq, both trained by IRGC Quds Force — attacked US forces with EFPs (Explosively Formed Penetrators), mortars, and rockets 2006–2008. The sophisticated EFPs, supplied by Iran, killed approximately 500 US troops. The attacks continued through the 2008 ceasefire imposed by Grand Ayatollah Sistani.

name: ISIS Blitzkrieg — Mosul and Northern Iraq (June 2014)
detail: In a stunning offensive, 1,500–3,000 ISIS fighters routed four Iraqi Army divisions (30,000+ nominal troops) and captured Mosul on June 10, 2014. Within 72 hours ISIS held Tikrit, Hawija, and Tal Afar. The Iraqi Army's collapse was attributed to corrupt officer corps, ghost soldiers, and Maliki's sectarian mismanagement. ISIS seized US military equipment including Humvees, artillery, and helicopters.

name: Iran-Aligned PMF Rocket Attacks on US Bases (2019–2024)
detail: Following the killing of Abu Mahdi al-Muhandis and Qasem Soleimani (January 2020), Iran-aligned PMF factions — particularly Kata'ib Hezbollah and affiliated groups — launched 300+ rocket and drone attacks on US bases in Iraq (Ain al-Assad, Erbil, Baghdad International Airport, Balad). Three US contractors killed October 2023; 160+ US troops with traumatic brain injuries from January 2020 Iran retaliation strikes. Attacks paused after US counter-strikes in February 2024.

name: Violent Suppression of Tishreen Protests (2019–2020)
detail: Iraq's security forces and Iran-aligned PMF factions killed 600+ and wounded 25,000+ protesters during the October 2019–January 2020 Tishreen Revolution. High-velocity tear gas launchers, live ammunition, and sniper fire were deployed against crowds. Subsequent campaign of targeted assassinations killed 100+ Tishreen activists and journalists through 2022, with near-total impunity for perpetrators. UNAMI attributed many killings to PMF-affiliated groups.
